results.name: licensing-and-certification-district-offices-california
results.notes:
This data contains a list of California Licensing and Certification District Offices. The California Department of Public Health, Center for Health Care Quality, Licensing and Certification (L&C) Program licenses and/or certifies more than 30 types of healthcare facilities in California. In addition, the Program contracts with the Los Angeles County (LA) to provide federal and state
survey and certification services throughout that area; LA is divided into 4 District Offices. The L&C District Office contact information is geocoded. 
The L&C Program is mandated to conduct various state re-licensing surveys to ensure that a provider is in compliance with all state laws and regulations by conducting on-site inspections (surveys) and investigating complaints and facility-reported events. The L&C Program is also contracted by the United States Department of Health and Human Services Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) to ensure that facilities accepting Medicare and Medi-Cal payments meet federal requirements. L&C is the largest program within CDPH, consisting of about 1,200 managers and staff located at headquarters in Sacramento and 14 district offices throughout the state. In addition, the Program contracts with the Los Angeles County (LA) to provide federal and state survey and certification services throughout that area; LA is divided into 4 District Offices. The L&C District Office contact information is geocoded.

organization.name: california-department-of-public-health
organization.title: California Department of Public Health
organization.description:
Website: https://cdph.ca.gov/

The California Department of Public Health (CDPH) works to protect the public's health in the Golden State and helps shape positive health outcomes for individuals, families and communities. The Department's programs and services, implemented in collaboration with local health departments and state, federal and private partners, touch the lives of every Californian and visitor to the state 24 hours a day, 7 days a week.

results.name: licensed-bed-classification-and-designations-trends
results.notes: This dataset contains information by licensed facility including number of patient days, hospital discharges, licensed bed days, and intra-hospital transfer from critical care by the six licensed bed classifications, including General Acute Care, Chemical Dependency Recovery, Acute Psychiatric, Skilled Nursing, Intermediate Care, and Intermediate Care/Developmentally Disabled.  The General Acute Care classification is further defined by bed designation categories that include Medical/Surgical (includes GYN/DOU), Perinatal (includes LDRP, excludes nursery), Pediatric Acute, Intensive Care, Coronary Care, Acute Respiratory Care, Burn Center, Intensive Care Newborn Nursery, and Rehabilitation Center bed designations.  2015 data are preliminary, are as reported and have not been audited.  See the Healthcare Facility Bed Types and Counts dataset for complimentary information at: https://chhs.data.ca.gov/Facilities-and-Services/Healthcare-Facility-Bed-Types-And-Counts/f9pb-6m2b

Medi-Cal Managed Care Capitation Rates for Capitated Rates for Senior Care Action Network (SCAN) – for Calendar Year 2016-2020. Senior Care Action Network (SCAN) Health Plan is a Medicare Advantage Special Needs Plan that contracts with the Department of Health Care Services for dual eligible Medicare/Medi-Cal population subset residing in Los Angeles, San Bernardino, and Riverside counties.

resources.name: All resource data

resources.description:
Medi-Cal Managed Care Capitation Rates for Senior Care Action Network (SCAN) – by Calendar Year 2020 

*  No MCO Tax
*  Long-Term Care Rate Changes – Retro & Prospective Combined - Adjustment to take into account the annual LTC rate changes.
*  Hospice Rate Increase - Retro & Prospective Combined - Adjustment to account for the increase to the hospice services rates, and hospice room and board rates; effective October and August, respectively, each year.
*  Optional Benefits Restoration - Adjustment to account for certain adult optional benefits, including vision (excluding lens fabrication), audiology, speech therapy, incontinence creams and washes, and podiatry, being restored as Medi-Cal covered benefits. Most of these benefits are already covered under SCAN's Medicare Advantage plans or under LTC. The only exception is the incontinence creams and washes, which are not Medicare Advantage-covered benefits.
resources.name: SCAN Rates CY 2020

resources.description:
Medi-Cal Managed Care Capitation Rates for Senior Care Action Network (SCAN) – by Calendar Year 2019 

*  Jan 2019 - Jun 2019 - MCO Tax Included 
*  Jul 2019 - Dec 2019 - MCO Tax Excluded
*  Long Term Care Rate Changes – Retro & Prospective Combined - Adjustment to take into account the annual LTC rate changes.
*  Hospice Rate Increase - Retro & Prospective Combined - Adjustment to account for the increase to the hospice services rates, and hospice room and board rates; effective October and August, respectively, each year.
resources.name: SCAN Rates CY 2019

resources.description:
Medi-Cal Managed Care Capitation Rates for Senior Care Action Network (SCAN) – by Calendar Year. 2018 

*  Includes MCO Tax
*  Long Term Care Rate Changes – Retro & Prospective Combined - Adjustment to take into account the annual LTC rate changes.
*  Hospice Rate Increase - Retro & Prospective Combined - Adjustment to account for the increase to the hospice services rates, and hospice room and board rates; effective October and August, respectively, each year.
resources.name: SCAN Rates CY 2018

resources.description:
Medi-Cal Managed Care Capitation Rates for Senior Care Action Network (SCAN) – by Calendar Year 2017 

*  Lower Bound rate percentage changes are based on July - December 2016 rates since the MCO tax for that rate period is similar to the CY 2017 rates. 
*  The Medical Transportation expenditures included in the base costs are overstated due to most capitated expenditures being covered by Medicare.  This adjustment removes all capitated expenditures of Medical Transportation expenditures except for some minimal amounts for LTC Certified COA and expenditures reimbursed based on FFS.
*  Dental Services are mostly covered by Medicare. Thus, an adjustment was made to remove all expenditures of Dental Service except for some minimal amounts for LTC Certified COA.
*  Long Term Care Rate Changes – Retro & Prospective Combined - Adjustment to take into account the annual LTC rate changes.
*  Hospice Rate Increase - Retro & Prospective Combined - Adjustment to account for the increase to the hospice services rates, and hospice room and board rates; effective October and August, respectively, each year.
*  Acupuncture - Effective July 1, 2016, acupuncture services were reinstituted as a Medi-Cal benefit.
*  Non-Medical Transportation - Non-medical transportation for managed care covered services has become managed care plans' responsibility effective July 1, 2017, and non-medical transportation for managed care not covered services has become managed care plans' responsibility effective October 1, 2017.
*  Medical Transportation - The Medical Transportation expenditures included in the base costs are overstated due to most capitated expenditures being covered by Medicare.  This adjustment removes all capitated expenditures of Medical Transportation expenditures except for some minimal amounts for LTC Certified COA and expenditures reimbursed based on FFS.
*  Dental Service - Dental Services are mostly covered by Medicare. Thus, an adjustment was made to remove all expenditures of Dental Service except for some minimal amounts for LTC Certified COA.
resources.name: SCAN Rates CY 2017

resources.description:
Medi-Cal Managed Care Capitation Rates for Senior Care Action Network (SCAN) – by Calendar Year 2016: 

o	3.9375% SB 78 Tax applied to 1/1/16-6/30/16 rates 

o	Enrollment-based MCO Tax applied to 7/1/16-12/31/16 

o	Includes Acupuncture as of 7/1/2016
resources.name: SCAN Rates CY 2016

results.name: eligible-individuals-enrolled-in-medi-cal-managed-care-cohs-health-plans
results.notes: This dataset includes the number of newly eligible individuals enrolled in a Medi-Cal Managed Care County Organized Health System (COHS) Health Plans by reporting period. COHS is a Medi-Cal managed care health plan model that operates in 22 California counties. Each COHS is created by a county board of supervisors and governed by an independent commission. In COHS counties, a single plan serves all Medi-Cal beneficiaries enrolled in managed care. This dataset is part of public reporting requirements set forth by the California Welfare and Institutions Code 14102.5.

This dataset displays reimbursement rates paid to Medi-Cal Managed Care health plans in the COHS model, by State Fiscal Year. The COHS serve beneficiaries in 22 counties. In the COHS model counties, the Department of Health Care Services (DHCS) contracts with a health plan created by the county Board of Supervisors.  In a COHS county, all Medi-Cal members are enrolled in the same managed care health plan.

resources.description:
This dataset displays reimbursement rates paid to Medi-Cal Managed Care health plans in the COHS model for Calendar Years 2021-2025. The COHS serves beneficiaries in 22 counties. In the COHS model counties, the Department of Health Care Services (DHCS) contracts with a health plan created by the county Board of Supervisors. In a COHS county, all Medi-Cal members are enrolled in the same managed care health plan.

__See Calendar Year (CY) tabs for changes throughout the years.__

__For archived data, please refer to the historical landing page at [Medi-Cal Managed Care Capitation Rates – County Organized Health Systems (COHS) Model](https://data.chhs.ca.gov/dataset/medi-cal-managed-care-capitation-rates-county-organized-health-systems-cohs-model) and [Medi-Cal Managed Care Supplemental Rates - Hepatitis C](https://data.chhs.ca.gov/dataset/medi-cal-managed-care-supplemental-rates-hepatitis-c)__

__For archived data, please refer to the historical landing page at [Medi-Cal Managed Care Capitation Rates - Geographic Managed Care (GMC)](https://data.chhs.ca.gov/dataset/medi-cal-managed-care-capitation-rates-geographic-managed-care-gmc) and [Medi-Cal Managed Care Supplemental Rates - Hepatitis C](https://data.chhs.ca.gov/dataset/medi-cal-managed-care-supplemental-rates-hepatitis-c)__

resources.description:
The dataset contains reimbursement rates paid to participating Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE) organizations for calendar years 2021-2024. To be eligible for the PACE program, a person must be 55 years of age or older and reside in one of the following PACE service areas: Alameda, Contra Costa, Fresno, Humboldt, Los Angeles, Orange, Riverside, Sacramento, San Bernardino, San Diego, San Francisco, San Joaquin, Santa Clara, Stanislaus.

__See Calendar Year tabs for changes throughout the years.__

__For archived data, please refer to the historical landing page at [Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE) Rates](https://data.chhs.ca.gov/dataset/program-of-all-inclusive-care-for-the-elderly-pace-rates)__
resources.name: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE) Rates

resources.description:
This dataset displays reimbursement rates paid to Medi-Cal Managed Care health plans in the Regional model, for Calendar Years 2021-2024. The Regional Model serves beneficiaries in 28 rural counties: Alpine, Amador, Butte, Calaveras, Colusa, Del Norte, El Dorado, Glenn, Humboldt, Inyo, Imperial, Lake, Lassen, Mariposa, Mendocino, Modoc, Mono, Nevada, Placer, Plumas, San Benito, Sierra, Siskiyou, Sutter, Tehama, Trinity, Tuolumne, and Yuba. The Department of Health Care Services (DHCS) contracts with two Commercial plans to provide services in each county. San Benito is the only exception where beneficiaries can choose either the managed care plan or (fee-for-service) Medi-Cal.

__See Calendar Year tabs for changes throughout the years.__

__For archived data, please refer to the historical landing page at [Medi-Cal Managed Care Capitation Rates – Regional Model/Rural Expansion](https://data.chhs.ca.gov/dataset/medi-cal-managed-care-capitation-rates-regional-model-rural-expansion)__
resources.name: Regional Model/Rural Expansion

__See Calendar Year tabs for changes throughout the years.__

results.name: long-term-care-facility-integrated-disclosure-and-medi-cal-cost-report-data-pivot-tables
results.notes: On an annual basis (based on individual Long-Term Care (LTC) facility fiscal year end), California licensed LTC facilities report detailed financial data on facility information, ownership information, patient days & discharges, Balance Sheet, Equity Statement, Cash Flows, Income Statement, Revenue by type and payer, Expense Detail,  and Labor Detail.  Based on the selected data set, the pivot tables display summarized data on a Profile page and also provides charts on various data items such as Patient Days, Revenue & Expense, and Revenue.

results.temporal: Data is collected and audited on an annual basis (individual facility fiscal year).  There will be a 5 year trend on most data sources,  however, you may search for other years on the HCAI website.

results.name: pre-2012-long-term-care-facility-integrated-disclosure-and-medi-cal-cost-report-data-pivot-tabl
results.notes: On an annual basis (based on individual Long-Term Care (LTC) facility fiscal year end), California licensed LTC facilities report detailed financial data on facility information, ownership information, patient days & discharges, Balance Sheet, Equity Statement, Cash Flows, Income Statement, Revenue by type and payer, Expense Detail, and Labor Detail. Based on the selected data set, the pivot tables display summarized data on a Profile page and also provides charts on various data items such as Patient Days, Revenue & Expense, and Revenue.

organization.name: department-of-health-care-access-and-information
organization.title: Department of Health Care Access and Information
organization.description:
Website: https://hcai.ca.gov/

Department of Health Care Access and Information (HCAI)  is committed to expanding equitable access to health care for all Californians—ensuring every community has the health workforce they need, safe and reliable health care facilities, and health information that can help make care more effective and affordable.

results.temporal: Data is collected and audited on an annual basis (individual facility fiscal year).  There will be a 5 year trend on most data sources,  however, you may search for other years on the HCAI website or by looking for a link within this dataset.
results.title: Pre-2012 Long-term Care Facility Integrated Disclosure and Medi-Cal Cost Report Data & Pivot Tables
